title: Breaking: Supreme Court Greenlights Alabama Mid-Decade Redistricting
summary:
In a 6-3 shadow docket order, and over a dissent by Justice Sotomayor, the Supreme Court effectively allowed Alabama to re-draw its congressional redistricting map. You can find the order here.
Following its decision in Louisiana v. Callais,… Continue reading 
The post Breaking: Supreme Court Greenlights Alabama Mid-Decade Redistricting appeared first on Election Law Blog.

title: Breaking: Virginia, in Longshot Bid Embracing Some Usual Republican Arguments, Goes to U.S. Supreme Court Asking It to Restore Democratic Gerrymander of Congressional Districts, Relying in Part Upon the Independent State Legislature Theory
summary:
From the Application:
A stay is warranted because the decision by the Supreme Court of Virginia is deeply mistaken on two critical issues of federal law with profound practical importance to the Nation. The decision below violates federal law… Continue reading
